mnemonics.title: Lock Modes Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: I SAY IX SIX: I-S, I-X, S, I-X, S-I-X, X - Remember IS comes before IX, SIX is combined S+IX, X is exclusive.

mnemonics.title: Compatibility Rule
mnemonics.content: 'I' locks (IS, IX) can play together; 'S' can play with 'I'; 'X' plays alone; 'SIX' is like S with X intention.

mnemonics.title: Lock Acquisition Order
mnemonics.content: SHOULD: Start from Root, Head down, Undo from Leaves, Lock Down, Release Up - S H O W (acquire) = Start from Hierarchy, Obtain locks in order, Watch parent before descending, Downward (acquire), Upward (release) = Lock Hierarchy Remembered

acronyms.usage: Indicates intention to read lower-level items in shared mode
Indicates intention to read and modify lower-level items
Holds shared lock on current node and intends to exclusively lock descendants
Locking technique allowing locks at different hierarchical levels
Lock that allows both reading and writing, granting exclusive access
Lock that allows reading but not modification

visualTips.visualization: Draw an inverted tree with Database at root, File/Table as intermediate nodes, Page as branch, and Record/Field as leaves. Imagine a pyramid structure.
Create a 6x6 grid. Fill diagonal with YES (self-compatible). IS row: YES to IS, S, IX; NO to SIX, X. IX row: YES to IS, IX; NO to S, SIX, X. X row: NO to all except NL.

Think of a hotel (database) with floors (files), rooms (pages), and beds (records). Intent locks are like a sign saying 'Guest staying in floor 3' so you know someone is in that floor without checking every room.

analogies.analogy: Coarse granularity is like reserving an entire hotel floor; fine granularity is like reserving just one room. More people can stay when individual rooms are reserved (higher concurrency), but managing room keys is harder (more overhead).
Intent locks are like a table of contents in a book - they tell you what chapters (lower levels) have important content (locks) without reading every page.
Think of locks as seating arrangements: S locks are like multiple people reading a book together (everyone can see); X lock is like someone using the book to write notes (needs privacy).
